Alma

A multimodal AI platform. Flask backend, React frontend, Gemini SDK. The macOS Swift app is the current preferred platform. Every feature exists because something else did not work.

Open Alma Source

Features

Each feature has its own page. Each page tells the real story of how it was built and how it broke.

  • Chat, the original feature. Send a message, get a response. It looks simple until you try to make it reliable.
  • Search, real-time web search with source citations. The feature that taught us CI is not your laptop.
  • Thinking, watch the model reason step by step. The feature that returned empty strings and called it success.
  • Voice, text-to-speech powered by gTTS. The feature with a race condition in the audio player.
  • Images, paste or drag images into the conversation. The feature that opened images in a new tab.
  • History, conversations persist locally. The feature that broke every E2E test after reload.

The macOS app

The Swift app is built with SwiftUI. It connects to the same Flask backend as the web frontend. It uses NavigationSplitView for the sidebar and detail view. The composer is a TextField with a send button. Messages render as Markdown.

The conversation service uses @Observable for state management. It handles create, select, rename, delete, and send operations. The settings view has a theme picker and keyboard shortcuts placeholder.

NavigationSplitView {
    SidebarView(service: service)
} detail: {
    ConversationView(service: service)
}

Keyboard shortcuts

Cmd + N    New conversation
/          Start a new conversation (web)
Ctrl + K   Open search (web)

Models

Uses Gemini via the Google AI SDK. The default model is selected automatically. The SDK raises ValueError for rate limits instead of returning a proper error response. We catch everything except programming errors and return synthetic text for recoverable failures.

Found something unexpected? Report an issue.
